current_status: Formal project F1-F8 have completed their current acceptance scope: gameplay runtime, pause/save/resume, death settlement, title-only meta progression purchasing, stable gameplay/UI scene resources, Settings persistence, formal SettingsPanel with only currently wired settings visible, runtime zh_CN/en UI refresh, keyboard-primary input rebinding, input binding UX polish, UIManager stack-top ui_back/default focus first slice, L1 smoke, Replay .replay roundtrip, replay-runner summary diff / runtime rerun, gameplay input recording, four checked-in golden replays, domain-separated SHA-256 RNG substream seed derivation with rng-audit correlation coverage, and perf-probe schema v2 comparable perf / balance baseline are in place. F8 current acceptance baseline passed closure audit and now serves as the regression guardrail for F9. F9 is now prepared via docs/AI协作/工作包/F9-ContentDemoPolish.md; ADR #82 changes the default aiming model from 4-direction aim to mouse direction aim with keyboard/controller fallback, and player/enemy placeholder facing is left/right only. ADR #83 adds the local data-package ModLoader interface: user://mods/<mod_id>/mod.json can declare JSON / CSV append patches and limited runtime contract extensions through DataLoader; Workshop integration remains future distribution-only work, with no player script execution in the current scope. ADR #84 adds the Steam-priority PlatformServices interface: business code should route Steam achievements, stats, rich presence, overlay, lobby / invite, and future platform APIs through PlatformServices; current backend remains none and no Steamworks SDK is connected. ADR #85 migrates the project engine and AI/tooling baseline from Godot 4.6.3 to Godot 4.7 after checking the official 4.7 migration guide against current code. ADR #86 hardens RNG stream seed mixing with a domain-separated SHA-256 mixer and adds rng-audit for cross-stream correlation checks. ADR #88 establishes the project IP direction as Nestbreakers / 破巢者: multi-hero runs are framed as one hero actively entering an enemy nest/core stronghold, stealing relic power, building synergies, and trying to break the nest core; nest is a broad enemy core / production source / will center, not only an insect hive. F9 now also has a debug/dev_tools-only DebugConsole and GMCommandRegistry first slice with help/stats/spawn/xp/hp/damage/heal/meta/kill/clear/seed commands, runtime double guard, and debug-tools-smoke / debug-tools-release-smoke coverage; real release export presets still need to exclude debug resources when packaging is created. F9.0 scope inventory is complete in docs/AI协作/工作包/F9-ContentDemoPolish.md. F9.1 small data-only content slice is complete: client/data/enemies.csv adds enemy_bulwark as a slow high-HP pressure enemy reusing the enemy_chaser pool/runtime, spawn_waves.csv adds wave_standard_mid_bulwarks from 55s onward, growth_pools.json adds growth_move_speed_small and growth_max_hp_small at min_level=3, game_modes.json includes enemy_bulwark in the standard enemy pool, locale strings cover new zh_CN/en text, and the four golden replay envelopes were updated to data_fingerprint b4138b1f767764bbafd3ef56d731e329567435c076b7a75dec498e15802dd302 while their runtime summaries still pass. F9.2 automated Demo preflight is in place via f9-demo-smoke and the user manually playtested the F9.1 slice with no issue reported, so no F9.1 numeric rebalance was applied. F9.3 first Demo presentation placeholder slice has landed: Enemy placeholders now draw a shared dark outline and eye outline on top of data-driven visual_color, without enemy-id branches or formal art assets. F9.3 Demo audio cue plan is documented, but the user confirmed there are no temporary audio resources now, so the current path is visual polish. Bullet and PickupOrb placeholders now also draw shared dark outlines, giving enemies / bullets / pickups a consistent temporary readability language. F9.3 feedback color-duration consistency also landed: Player has a dark outline and 0.16s red hurt flash, Enemy hit uses a 0.16s warm-white flash, Enemy death uses a 0.18s orange scale/fade, and GameplayHud upgrade feedback uses gold text, dark shadow, and a 1.35s fade. F9.3 hit spark / damage-number pools now also landed: GameplayRunLoop registers and prewarms hit_spark and damage_number, listens to Combat.damage_applied, and spawns short-lived pooled feedback only when damage is actually applied; these feedback nodes do not enter run snapshots. The user manually checked F9.3 floating-number density / occlusion and reported no issue, so no feedback numeric tuning is needed now. F9.4 automatic regression / perf guardrails passed: baseline gates, core smoke, four checked-in golden replay runtime reruns, and perf-probe all passed without replay recapture. F9.5 complete Demo manual checklist also passed by user confirmation, so the first F9 Demo pass is closed. A detailed optional feature suggestion pool now exists at docs/功能建议池.md with 42 candidate feature directions; it is a manual selection menu, not an accepted roadmap or implementation authorization. The next step is to wait for the user to choose a feature from docs/功能建议池.md, then create or update the corresponding work package / GDD / ADR / module docs and implement only the chosen scope. AudioManager registration / smoke stays deferred until temporary audio resources exist. Stable gameplay/UI structure should continue to default to .tscn resources, with scripts focused on node binding, data refresh, and signal flow; dynamic node creation remains limited to object pools and data-driven repeated rows/buttons.
